mission.title: Install and Configure Redis Operator (Spotahome / OT-CONTAINER-KIT)
mission.description: The Redis Operator by OT-CONTAINER-KIT simplifies the deployment and management of Redis instances on Kubernetes, supporting standalone, cluster, and sentinel modes. It provides built-in monitoring and adheres to best practices for security and performance. This operator is ideal for users looking to efficiently manage Redis workloads in a Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Add Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Redis Operator Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add redis-operator https://ot-container-kit.github.io/redis-operator

helm repo update

```

> This adds the official repository for the Redis Operator.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Redis Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Redis Operator in the specified namespace, ensuring all necessary resources are created.

```bash

kubectl create namespace redis-operator

helm install redis-operator redis-operator/redis-operator --namespace redis-operator --version 0.24.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Redis Operator.
> Installs the Redis Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a Redis Cluster Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource (CR) that defines the desired state of your Redis cluster.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: redis.opstree.dev/v1
kind: Redis
metadata:
  name: my-redis
  namespace: redis-operator
spec:
  size: 3
  redis:
    image: redis:7.0.0
EOF

```

> Creates a Redis cluster with 3 replicas.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: redis.opstree.dev/v1
kind: Redis
metadata:
  name: my-redis
  namespace: redis-operator
spec:
  size: 3
  redis:
    image: redis:7.0.0
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Redis Cluster
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Redis cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get redis -n redis-operator

kubectl describe redis my-redis -n redis-operator

```

> Lists all Redis resources in the redis-operator namespace.
> Describes the Redis resource to check its status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Redis Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Redis Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all Redis instances managed by the operator, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall redis-operator --namespace redis-operator

kubectl delete namespace redis-operator

```

> Uninstalls the Redis Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Redis Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Redis data. This step will upgrade the Redis Operator to a newer version while maintaining your existing configurations.

```bash

kubectl get redis -n redis-operator -o yaml > redis-backup.yaml

helm upgrade redis-operator redis-operator/redis-operator --namespace redis-operator --version 0.24.0

kubectl get redis -n redis-operator

```

> Backs up the current Redis configurations.
> Upgrades the Redis Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Redis instances are still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get redis -n redis-operator -o yaml > redis-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create Redis instance'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration in the Custom Resource.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits and ensure the cluster has enough CPU and memory allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Redis instance not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Redis pods are not starting due to image pull errors or insufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and check for image pull errors.

troubleshooting.title: Monitoring not available
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The redis-exporter may not be deployed or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the redis-exporter is included in your Redis Custom Resource configuration.

troubleshooting.title: TLS connection errors
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the TLS settings in the Redis configuration are incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the TLS settings in your Redis Custom Resource and ensure certificates are correctly mounted.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Redis 7.0, improved monitoring capabilities with redis-exporter, and enhanced TLS configuration options.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.19 or earlier, review the new configuration options for Redis 7.0 compatibility.

resolution.summary: You should see the Redis instances listed with 'kubectl get redis -n redis-operator', showing their status as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: redis.opstree.dev/v1
kind: Redis
metadata:
  name: my-redis
  namespace: redis-operator
spec:
  size: 3
  redis:
    image: redis:7.0.0
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
